item.content:encoded:
So this is the somewhat obligatory post in acknowledgement of this article being rated over 100 (which I waited for it to be rated +101, because I can). This has always been among my more successful works, and the one with the shortest time between initial ideation to posting (about one week).

I am glad that it got what it deserved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not liking the link to 2165-J, as that isn't an "actual" joke article.

Firstly, it is actually SCP-2615-J. There is no article called SCP-2165-J (at time of writing).

I still do defend the use of SCP-2615-J, even if it is a false -J article. This crosslink adds prespective and allots a place for -J SCPs in the Foundation, while still implying the sense of seriousness within the Foundation (an element that SCP-2615-J and SCP-2615 can present). Had it been another -J article, I feel that the gravitas would be lost and it would simply be a case of showing a random -J article for the lolz.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not liking the link to 2165-J, as that isn't an "actual" joke article. I think that the link should be replaced with another article, like SCP-1000-J or SCP-2130-J.
The article gets a +1 though.

item.title: Very nice.
item.content:encoded: I caught the reference really quickly, but I still enjoyed reading it. Have an upvote.

item.title: Re: Thanks, Explanation and Other Matters
item.content:encoded:
…and camera-based tsukumogami are impossible since tsukumogami don't go well with electricity (or electrical appliances, in general).

Actually, I've had some experience with tsukumogami. Turns out the whole "tsukumogami spirits are repelled by electricity" aspect of the lore is a complete load of crap (as is every variant of the "magic & science don't get along" cliche that I've had any level of first-hand experience with). It's just that by the time an electronic device meets all the criteria for conversion into a tsukumogami creature, it tends to be in too deplorable of a condition for the conversion process to work (for lack of a better way of putting it).

item.title: Cool
item.content:encoded:
This is a cool SCP with an interesting mythological tie-in.

Also, it reminded me of an old sci-fi short story which I can't find anymore. It was about a group of homeless people that discover aliens that disguise themselves as useless items. there's a line which says something like 'I saw a dozen straight razors crawling out of a hole in the side [of their spaceship]' Does that sound familiar to anyone?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think you'd actually weaken this article if you fleshed out its origins and surrounding lore. The lack of backstory encourages people to think up their own mutually-exclusive origins for the object, which builds on the "headcanon" metahumour.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this. No killing people or global destruction, just experiments and science — what I feel the Foundation's all about.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I miss SCP-damej-J. Especially the audio file (and its head cannon).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For all its worth, it was inspired by a now-deleted -J SCP (damej-J). When I heard that bit on "head cannon", I thought I must make something on that bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is incredibly on the nose and kinda feels like it would be a slightly better fit for the -J list, even if it had to compete with other headcannons. But then again, one of my favorite humourous mainlisters also is precisely so. I had fun reading it, so I guess you earn your +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm honoured for you to take your time through this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hi Mr Wrong,

After rereading your SCP and analyzing the concept, I really need to say that your work is great.
It deserves an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this, it's one of my favorite kinds of SCPs. A simple effect with lots of thought out follow-through and interesting details. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And as it turned out, I gave in and amended the numbering issue with those less than 10.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was mostly just intrigued by the end, the recovery and final logs especially. But reading the spoilers, this is just so cool. It's interesting enough on its own merits, but what went on behind creating it is so clever. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The general rule for spelling out versus using numerals is to spell out numbers less than ten (though some will push this up to 20, as that's where you'd have to start hyphenating) and anything over ten, to use numerals for.

Seven maids a-milking and 12 lords a-leaping, just as an example.

Edit: and if I'd read sirpudding's post correctly, I could have foregone mine completely. Oops.

item.title: Re: Thanks, Explanation and Other Matters
item.content:encoded: Doing a quick search, I daresay this is the only article (as per 25 June 2015) that references Hunter x Hunter… of all things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you. I was actually slightly lost after I came up with "Word of God"… until I opted to insert a god inside the story.

+ show block
– hide block

For those who would wonder "Why isn't this god in the story?", I don't know if I want to flesh him out… It started out as a meta-joke… but I may do a mini-series about Indra's net if I get ideas on how to do the rest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In the original draft I read, I was put off a bit by the reliance on the obscure meta joke. I'm glad to see that you have not only made the meta joke a bit more explicit, but also have expanded the object outside the meta joke.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I was reading this and groaned, thinking,
spoilers
it's a headcanon
and I was right.

No vote.

item.title: Re: g/cm³ >:(
item.content:encoded:
If you're referring to the uncollapsed procedures, I would say that they are drafted in after the events of Addendum 2159-4 (with "the present" being post Addendum 2159-4). The real procedures (relevant to the story) are in the previous versions.

In that light (and from my understanding), was it confusing (in a bad way) that the more relevant1 procedures are hidden under a collapsible while less relevant procedures are put up on the front?

Footnotes
1. Relevance to the narrative

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's an ^vote from me. It's kooky and I like kooky things and it has simple, yet interesting narrative.

item.title: Re: g/cm³ >:(
item.content:encoded: The tone in the special containment procedures feels like there is less than you actually wrote which is the reason I dislike your procedures. Anyway I changed my mind (neutral vote), I really need to reread it, I find it a complex SCP.

item.title: Re: Thanks, Explanation and Other Matters
item.content:encoded: You don't see many Hunter X Hunter references on this site. Bravo.

item.title: Re: g/cm³ >:(
item.content:encoded:
but I dislike your tone.
I think you could do that better.

Since you had mentioned tone and a desire for it be improved, in what way do they feel jarring?

item.title: g/cm³ >:(
item.content:encoded:
The structure is suitable for the idea, but I dislike your tone.
I think you could do that better.
Downvote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's true, although my pet peeve is to keep things consistent. I simply cannot stand some of my numbers being words while others are in numerals.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Edit: One grammar thing: I would say between rather than among the two personnel, as there are only two in question.

I went with "among the personnel" since I did state "at least 2 personnel".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought the preferred style was words instead of numerals for counting numbers less than 10 ie. "two persons" instead of "2 persons".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Edit: One grammar thing: I would say between rather than among the two personnel, as there are only two in question.

I liked it. I enjoyed the canon/guan yin references without really paying attention the the meta. (That Pachelbel reference was cheesy.)

Ultimately, I upvoted at the end based on the final form of Iteration 0. See, I don't see it as a contained object that has reached a final state of equilibrium. I see it as some kind of supernatural expression of divine chaos, and the fact that the Foundation has isolated it and whittled it down to a specific description is probably actually pretty detrimental to reality.

That's my headcanon, anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Grammar issues have bee noted and amended.

Finally, the mentions of electrical buildup seem out of place and it doesn't lend itself to the rest of the item.

It was intended to allude to its true nature as a lightning bolt-diamond, that it is still an charged (as in, electrical charge) object. It's designed to look out-of-place, hinting on the true nature of the item's natural state.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Novote for now. It's certainly clever and I like the stealth puns you slipped in, but the whole time I was reading it I was waiting for the other shoe to drop. It seems like one of those items that you expect to suddenly become very unnerving by the end, but it just keeps doing the same thing. I felt underwhelmed. Maybe that's just me.

Two grammar notes:

no two individuals would perceive SCP-2159 as the same object.

Photographs and video footage of SCP-2159 display also display this anomalous property

Finally, the mentions of electrical buildup seem out of place and it doesn't lend itself to the rest of the item.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 for a thing what required applied macro-scale superposition to contain.

Edit: Also, totally agree with Petrograd that this thing isn't contained, and the foundation attempt to contain it may have dented reality a little. Which I think suits it: it is now either a mostly neutralized contained object and subtle meta-reference, or an extremely dangerous anomalous item that is capable of anything up to and including breaching the fourth wall to prevent itself from being defined as a single thing.

item.title: Thanks, Explanation and Other Matters
item.content:encoded:
Allow me to extend my thanks to various people: wheatmiser, TyGently, Dr_Leonard, AndarielHalo among others.

This article references the following articles:

  • SCP-1132-J (Head-Cannons) by Aelanna
  • SCP-2615-J (Clap Your Hands) by DreamwalkerFae
  • SCP-damej-J (damej robit) by Faminepulse, djkaktus, and Decibelles

And what is the damned thing anyway…

+ show block
– hide block

All the iterations share a common theme: "headcanon". SCP-2159 is the incarnation of headcanon, so everyone perceives it as headcanon.

Isaac Netero fights via something called the Type-100 Guan Yin Bodhisattva, while Graduation is based on Canon in D.

However, over time, these headcanons become superseded by Word of God that incorporates the opinion of the so-called "fanbase" into the finalised canon.

Iteration 0 is technically a vajra, a ritualistic item that represents both lightning and diamond. That is the explanation for some of the traits surrounding SCP-2159 (density, fulgurite etc.). It literally fell to the Earth, that's for sure.

As for the unexplained context… let's just say that a (trickster?) deity wants to make a point about perception.

And Halo, I'm sorry. I will admit that something sentient and with souls can be affected. However, I find hard to believe that AI technology would be that advanced by 2004 and camera-based tsukumogami are impossible since tsukumogami don't go well with electricity (or electrical appliances, in general).
